mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE rises >0.2pp, or directional accuracy falls below 48%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with out-of-sample directional accuracy below 52% over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices indicate expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days specifically during early-cycle recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases yield negative 20-day returns during post-shock recovery periods, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC < 0.01 outside this regime.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects sustained labor market expansion, supporting consumer spending expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward returns across a 5-year rolling window, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 300, inflation expectations force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 300 are followed by positive 60-day returns in >60% of cases, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirms synchronized demand expansion, driving earnings upgrades and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, with out-of-sample directional accuracy below 50% over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during late-cycle expansions signals supply bottlenecks and impending economic slowdown, triggering immediate profit-taking and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle expansions are followed by positive 5-day returns in >55% of instances, or the signal shows no regime-dependent directional bias.
